subject: Drilbur, a small mole-like Pokemon with prominent claws, depicted in clay-figurine art style on a dirt ground
description: A small, rounded mole-like creature crouches low on a bed of brown dirt and scattered pebbles. The creature has a dark blue-grey body, a cream or white face and underbelly, small circular eyes, and large forward-pointing claws used for digging. The art has a distinct clay or sculpted tactile quality — soft shadows, rounded forms, and a matte texture — characteristic of the artist Yuka Morii. The composition is a low-angle close-up that emphasizes the creature's digging posture.
foreground: small mole-like creature with dark body and cream belly, crouching with prominent claws extended
emotion: low and alert, digging or ready-to-dig posture
